pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 17-95 show this file is generated by the task setup script
task.json description and export_result.sh confirm this output path
task.json metadata and verifier.py line 36 confirm 8KB minimum

task.json metadata specifies min_h1: 7, verifier.py line 80 checks this threshold. Task description implies ~7 main sections (Title, ToC, Introduction, Functional Reqs, Non-Functional Reqs, External Interfaces, Appendix)
task.json metadata specifies min_h2: 10, verifier.py line 90 checks this. Task requires 5 modules + 4 NFR categories = 9 minimum, with room for additional subsections
task.json metadata and verifier.py line 111. With 5 functional modules, at least 5 requirement tables are needed, exceeding the minimum of 4
task.json metadata and verifier.py line 139. With 25 functional requirements + 4 non-functional requirements + introduction/description text, 40+ paragraphs is achievable
setup_task.sh JSON contains exactly 5 modules: FR-AUTH, FR-APPT, FR-MR, FR-MSG, FR-BILL (verified by code analysis)

task.json lists 'HIPAA' in required_keywords, but analysis of setup_task.sh JSON shows HIPAA does not appear anywhere in the generated patientconnect_requirements.json. Only PatientConnect, FHIR, and Pinnacle are present.
setup_task.sh JSON contains 'PatientConnect' in document_metadata.title (line 21) and project_info.name (line 27)
setup_task.sh JSON contains 'FHIR' in multiple functional requirements: FR-APPT-01 (line 45), FR-MR-01 (line 55), and external_interfaces (line 90)
install_openoffice.sh line 73 specifies version 4.1.16 released November 2025. Verified through Apache OpenOffice official release notes
task.json description and export_result.sh line 11 confirm .odt output format
setup_task.sh lines 15-95 show the JSON data is created inline using cat heredoc, not downloaded from external source

pi_items.verified_value: 13 healthcare/IT standards referenced: FHIR (3x), SAML (1x), HL7 (2x), NIST (1x), C-CDA (1x), WCAG (1x), X12 (1x), PCI DSS (1x), AES (1x), DICOM (1x), RxNorm (1x), USCDI (1x), 21st Century Cures Act (1x)
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh JSON content analysis. All standards verified as real via web search (HL7, FHIR, Epic EHR, Twilio, Stripe confirmed as legitimate healthcare systems)

privileged_info_summary: This task uses Apache OpenOffice Writer 4.1.16 to create an FRS document for a synthetic healthcare project called 'PatientConnect 3.0'. The input data is generated by the setup script and contains 5 functional requirement modules (Authentication, Scheduling, Medical Records, Messaging, Billing), 4 non-functional requirements, and 3 external system interfaces (Epic EHR, Twilio, Stripe Connect). The metadata specifies minimum structural requirements: 7 Heading 1 sections, 10 Heading 2 subsections, 4 tables, 40+ paragraphs, and must include keywords: PatientConnect, FHIR, HIPAA, and Pinnacle. CRITICAL FINDING: The required_keywords list includes 'HIPAA' but the generated JSON data does not contain this term anywhere - it only contains PatientConnect, FHIR, and Pinnacle. The document output path is /home/ga/Documents/FRS-PC3-2024-012.odt with a minimum file size of 8KB. All healthcare standards and external systems referenced (FHIR, HL7, Epic, Twilio, Stripe) are real and commonly used in healthcare applications.
